 {"id":"bd-14la","title":"Allocation Budget: Sequential Leak Detection (e-process + CUSUM)","description":"## Goal
Detect allocation regressions/leaks in reflow with formal, anytime-valid guarantees and actionable diagnostics.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Track allocation counts/bytes per frame as a time series. Use:
- CUSUM for quick drift detection (mean shift).
- An e-process for anytime-valid testing to avoid false positives under optional stopping.

Example e-process for residuals r_t (mean 0 under null):
E_t = product exp(lambda * r_t - (lambda^2 * sigma^2)/2). Trigger if E_t > 1/alpha.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detect sustained allocation drift within <= N frames for target deltas.
- False positive rate <= alpha on stable runs.
- Debug output includes evidence ledger: r_t, E_t, thresholds, and suspected callsites.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_eprocess_threshold: triggers at E_t > 1/alpha.
- unit_cusum_detects_shift: mean shift detected under drift.\n- property_fpr_control: empirical FPR <= alpha + tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Synthetic leak injection: verify detection and logs.
- Stable run: no alerts, E_t stays bounded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:05.146984605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:54:32.204779375Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:54:32.204757774Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.3","title":"Showcase integration for Log Search","description":"## Integration Plan
- Add search UI to showcase log screen (input line + mode toggles)
- Bind `/` to open search, `Esc` to close
- Display match count + current mode in status line

## Tests
- Snapshot: search bar open/closed
- PTY E2E: open search, type, toggle regex, exit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Integration does not flicker in inline mode
- [ ] Help overlay lists search keys

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1rzr","title":"Action Timeline: Alert Threshold Calibration (Conformal + e-process)","description":"## Goal
Set change-point alert thresholds with formal false-positive control and provide an explanation ledger per alert.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Conformal thresholding on residuals from the sketch baseline:
- Given calibration residuals R = {r_1..r_n}, threshold q = quantile_{1-alpha} using (n+1) rule.
- Alert when r_t > q.

Anytime-valid layer:
- Maintain an e-process on residuals to allow early alerts without inflating false positives.
- Example: e_t = exp(lambda * (r_t - mu) - (lambda^2 * sigma^2)/2), E_t = product e_t.
- Trigger if E_t > 1/alpha.

## Acceptance Criteria
- FPR <= alpha on synthetic and recorded traces.
- Alert carries evidence ledger: residual, q, conformal score, e-value, alpha.
- No alert spam under steady-state traffic.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_conformal_threshold: threshold equals (1-alpha) quantile of calibration set.
- unit_evalue_monotone: e-process nonnegative and grows with evidence.
- property_fpr_control: empirical FPR <= alpha + tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Replay trace with known change points; log alert timing and evidence.

- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.

 {"id":"bd-b8m5","title":"Define CapturedError struct for panic information","description":"# Define CapturedError struct for panic information

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). When a widget panics, we need to capture comprehensive diagnostic information for debugging.

## Technical Design

```rust
use std::any::Any;
use std::backtrace::Backtrace;
use std::time::Instant;

/// Captured error from a widget panic
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct CapturedError {
    /// Error message extracted from panic
    pub message: String,
    
    /// Name of the widget that panicked
    pub widget_name: &'static str,
    
    /// Backtrace at panic point (if available)
    pub backtrace: Option<String>,
    
    /// When the panic occurred
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    
    /// Area the widget was rendering when it panicked
    pub area: Rect,
    
    /// Render phase when panic occurred
    pub phase: RenderPhase,
    
    /// Parent widget chain (for context)
    pub widget_path: Vec<&'static str>,
}

/// Phase of rendering when error occurred
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum RenderPhase {
    /// During layout/constraint solving
    Layout,
    /// During actual buffer writes
    Render,
    /// During event handling
    Update,
}

impl CapturedError {
    /// Create from a caught panic
    pub fn from_panic(
        panic_info: Box<dyn Any + Send>,
        widget_name: &'static str,
        area: Rect,
        phase: RenderPhase,
    ) -> Self {
        // Extract message from panic payload
        let message = if let Some(s) = panic_info.downcast_ref::<&str>() {
            s.to_string()
        } else if let Some(s) = panic_info.downcast_ref::<String>() {
            s.clone()
        } else {
            "Unknown panic".to_string()
        };
        
        // Capture backtrace
        let backtrace = match std::backtrace::Backtrace::capture().status() {
            std::backtrace::BacktraceStatus::Captured => {
                Some(std::backtrace::Backtrace::capture().to_string())
            }
            _ => None,
        };
        
        Self {
            message,
            widget_name,
            backtrace,
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            area,
            phase,
            widget_path: Vec::new(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Add parent widget to path (for nested boundaries)
    pub fn with_parent(mut self, parent: &'static str) -> Self {
        self.widget_path.push(parent);
        self
    }
    
    /// Format for logging
    pub fn log_format(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "Widget panic in {}: {} at {:?}",
            self.widget_name,
            self.message,
            self.area
        )
    }
    
    /// Format for user display (truncated)
    pub fn display_format(&self, max_len: usize) -> String {
        let msg: String = self.message.chars().take(max_len.saturating_sub(4)).collect();
        format!("[!] {}...", msg)
    }
}
```

## Why This Design
1. **Rich context**: Widget name, area, phase all captured
2. **Backtrace optional**: May not be available in all builds
3. **Widget path**: Shows nesting for complex UIs
4. **Two formats**: Full for logs, truncated for display

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CapturedError struct with all fields
- [ ] from_panic extracts message from Any
- [ ] Backtrace captured when available
- [ ] log_format() for tracing
- [ ] display_format() for UI","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:16.386273542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:09.284105824Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:09.284072601Z","close_reason":"CapturedError struct already implemented as part of bd-1euu in ftui-widgets/src/error_boundary.rs with message, widget_name, area, timestamp fields and from_panic constructor","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b8m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1euu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:40.505924462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-bksf","title":"Demo Showcase: Mouse/Hit-Test Playground","description":"## Background

Mouse support in terminals is notoriously inconsistent (modes, encodings, multiplexers). FrankenTUI supports rich input parsing, and this screen exists to:
- prove correctness of mouse event decoding (SGR, scroll, drag)
- prove correctness of hit-testing and hover state
- provide a playground to tune jitter/hysteresis policies

It should feel like a "mouse lab" with immediate visual feedback.

## UX / Core Interactions

- A canvas of interactive widgets:
  - buttons, scrollbars, draggable sliders
  - a small grid of hit-test targets to test edge boundaries
- Live event readout:
  - last N mouse events (type, coords, modifiers)
  - current hover target ID
- Optional overlay:
  - draw hit-test boxes and hovered region outline

Suggested keybindings:
- O: toggle hit-test overlay
- J: toggle jitter stabilization visualization
- C: clear event log

## Architecture Notes

- Drive events from ftui-core mouse parsing.
- Hit-test index + dirty-rect cache are validated by bd-2qag.
- Hover jitter stabilization policy validated by bd-9n09.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Demonstrates all major mouse event classes:
  - down/up for left/middle/right
  - move
  - drag
  - scroll up/down
- Hover state is stable (no flicker) when the pointer micro-jitters.
